lessons.title: Variables & Data Types
lessons.subtitle: The Banana Counter (Senthil & Goundamani)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Goundamani lines up a question: "Rendu banana enga?". In Python, variables are like Senthil's pockets holding values. Senthil pocket-la value store panraaru: bananas = 2. Senthil oru banana saptutaaru, so: bananas = bananas - 1. Variable-na enna theriyuma? Oru data-va store panni vechikra box!
lessons.english_exp: Goundamani asks: "Where are the two bananas?". In Python, variables are named locations used to store data in memory. For example, bananas = 2 stores the number 2. When Senthil eats one, bananas = bananas - 1 updates the value stored to 1.
lessons.initial_code:
# Senthil's Banana variable
bananas = 2
bananas_eaten = 1

remaining = bananas - bananas_eaten
print("Remaining bananas:")
print(remaining)

quiz.question:
If we run this code, what will be stored in the variable 'x'?
x = 100
x = x - 25
quiz.correct_headline: Correct! Romba fast-ah kandu pudichitiye pa!
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Ahaaa, oru ruba kooda kuraiyama 75 ruba! Naan nenaicha maadhiriye panra pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
Oru ruba kooda kuraiyama correct!
quiz.incorrect_text: Goundamani: "Rendu banana-la onna saptutu, mathadhu enga da nu ketta, 25-nu solra? Enna da calculation idhu!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😠
😅
Rendu banana enga da?
Athu... mathadhu logic illa anna!

lessons.title: Conditionals (If-Else)
lessons.subtitle: Naanum Rowdy Thaan (Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Decision points are handled using if and else. Vadivelu rowdy status-a check panraaru. Rowdy template condition: If weapon is True, write: "Naanum Rowdy Thaan!", else: "Sir, naan simple citizen sir!". Python code structure is very clean, it uses spaces (indentation) instead of curly brackets!
lessons.english_exp: Conditional statements perform different actions based on whether a boolean condition evaluates to True or False. Python uses if, elif, and else. Code blocks are grouped using indentation (4 spaces).

quiz.question: What will print if is_rowdy = False in the code template above?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Escape aayitaanda! Naan normal citizen nu solli safe-a poitaan."
quiz.correct_svg:
😏
Safe-a escape aayitaan!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Aiyo, rowdy illenu sonnalum build-up koduthu lock aayitiye da!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😭
Vandiyile yethungada ivana!

lessons.title: Loops (For & While)
lessons.subtitle: The Endless Tea Shop Tasks (Vivekh & Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Repeated operations are called Loops. Goundamani Senthil-ku tea loop check panraaru. If a task needs to run 3 times, we use for i in range(3):. Infinite loop potteenga-na system crash aagum, Vadivelu-ku adi thidirnu vizhunde kittu irukum!
lessons.english_exp: Loops are used to iterate over a sequence (list, tuple, string) or repeat actions. A for loop iterates over a range or collection, while a while loop runs as long as a condition is True.
lessons.expected_output:
Adi vizhugiathu!
Adi vizhugiathu!
Adi vizhugiathu!

quiz.question:
How many times will this loop print 'Aiyo'?
for i in range(5):
print('Aiyo')
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "Innuma indha ooru 5 thadava adi vangumbothu loops-a nambitu iruku? Ha ha!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
Aanandam... vilaiyaadum veedu!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Valikudhu! Range calculation thappa pochu. Innum extra adi vilugudhe!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Ennada 0/4-nu calculation solrae!

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.subtitle: Commanding the Sidekicks (Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Functions are block of codes that run only when called. Vadivelu instructions-a compile panni oru plan template create panraaru: def attack_plan():. function definition kuduthutu appram call panninaa thaan execution nadakkum. attack_plan() call panra varai zero performance!
lessons.english_exp: Functions are reusable blocks of code. They are defined using the def keyword, followed by the function name, parentheses, and a colon. You invoke them by writing the function name followed by parentheses.
lessons.initial_code:
def attack_plan():
    print("Build up jaasthi, performance zero!")

# Call the function!
attack_plan()

quiz.question: Which keyword is used to declare a function in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Def keyword-a correct-a select pannitiye! Unakku attack plan ready-a iruku!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😏
Nalla plan! Appidiye follow pannu!
quiz.incorrect_text: Santhanam: "Function-nu Javascript style-la Python-la type panra paaru! Enna oru puthisaalithanam!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🤦‍♂️
Aiyo! JS-laam Python-la ezhuthaadha pa!

lessons.title: Lists & Indexing
lessons.subtitle: Tea Shop Menu Card (Goundamani & Senthil)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Lists allow storing multiple items in a single variable. Menu card logic: menu = ["tea", "vadai", "bun"]. Items-a access panna Index range 0-la irundhu start aagum. So menu[0]-na tea, menu[1]-na vadai!
lessons.english_exp: Lists are used to store multiple items in a single variable. Lists are ordered, changeable, and allow duplicate values. Items are indexed, with the first item having index [0].
lessons.initial_code:
menu = ["tea", "vadai", "bun"]
print("First item:")
print(menu[0])
print("Second item:")
print(menu[1])
lessons.expected_output:
First item:
tea
Second item:
vadai

quiz.question: What will menu[2] print in the menu list above?
quiz.correct_text: Goundamani: "Correct-a bun-nu sonna unaku oru special tea track ready!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😛
Bun-nu correct-a solidhaanda!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "Tea-nu solreenga, illana vadai-nu solreenga. Index correct-a parkalaye anna!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Bun thaanga index 2!

lessons.title: Strings & Slice Operations
lessons.subtitle: Enna Vechu Comedy Pannalaye (Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Strings-na text characters combination box! Python-la strings sliced operations and reversal mega easy: text[::-1] potta text thalaigeezhaga reverse aayidum! text.upper() potta Vadivelu kuthu shouting text maadhiri ALL CAPS-la maarum!
lessons.english_exp: Strings in Python are arrays of bytes representing unicode characters. Python supports powerful slicing using string[start:stop:step] syntax. For instance, text[::-1] reverses a string.
lessons.initial_code:
hero = "Vadivelu"
print("Original name:", hero)
print("Uppercase shout:", hero.upper())
print("Reversed string:", hero[::-1])
lessons.expected_output:
Original name: Vadivelu
Uppercase shout: VADIVELU
Reversed string: ulevidaV

quiz.question: What does 'python'[::-1] output in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Ahaaa! Reverse slicing-la thalaigeezhaga correct-a solidiye pa! nohtyp!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
nohtyp! Super slicing!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Enna vechu comedy pannalaye! Slicing step -1 thalaigeezha poga sonna, straight-a padikiraye da!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Step -1 reversed string boss!

lessons.title: Dictionaries & Key-Value Pairs
lessons.subtitle: Anniyan Split Persona (Kamal & Vikram)
lessons.tanglish_exp: List-la Index 0, 1, 2 thaan irukum. Aana Dictionaries-la key-value mapping irukum! Like: actor = {"name": "Vadivelu", "role": "Rowdy"}. Key-va kudutha direct value unlock aagum: actor["role"] ➔ Rowdy!
lessons.english_exp: Dictionaries are used to store data values in key:value pairs. A dictionary is a collection which is ordered, changeable, and does not allow duplicates. Retrieve values using `dict[key]`.
lessons.initial_code:
# Character dictionary
actor = {
    "name": "Vadivelu",
    "dialogue": "Naanum Rowdy Thaan!",
    "rank": 1
}
print(actor["name"])
print(actor["dialogue"])

quiz.question: How do you access the value of key 'role' in hero = {'name': 'Rajini', 'role': 'Chitti'}?
quiz.correct_text: Chitti: "Speed 1 Terahertz! Key 'role' access panni 'Chitti' get pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
Speed 1 Terahertz correct!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "Anna, List Indexing maadhiri Dictionary key access panraare anna! KeyError vandhudume!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
dict['key'] nu format-la thaan tharanum!

lessons.title: File Handling (Open & Read)
lessons.subtitle: Secret Document Reader (Vadivelu & Vivek)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Files-a Python script valiya open panni read/write panna open() or with open() as f: use aagum. `with` statement use panna task mudinjadhum file automatically close aayidum! Memory leak escape!
lessons.english_exp: File handling in Python is performed using the built-in open() function. Using with open('file.txt', 'r') as f: ensures the file is automatically closed after operations.
lessons.initial_code:
# Simulating writing and reading a secret file
secret_content = "Vadivelu Attack Plan: Step 1 Escape!"
print("File Data:")
print(secret_content)
lessons.expected_output:
File Data:
Vadivelu Attack Plan: Step 1 Escape!

quiz.question: Which Python keyword ensures a file is automatically closed after reading?
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "'with' block potta file close aagum, memory leaks escape aagum! Professional coding!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
Memory leaks clean escape!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "File open panni vachutu close panna marandhuteenga-na resource lock aayidum boss!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😰
'with open()' keyword try pannunga!

lessons.title: Exception Handling (Try-Except)
lessons.subtitle: Enna Kodumai Sir Idhu! (Prabhu & Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Code-la error vandha app crash aagaama handle panna try and except use aagum! Divide by Zero (`10 / 0`) pannina ZeroDivisionError varum. except ZeroDivisionError: potta Vadivelu safe-a escape aayiruvaaru!
lessons.english_exp: Exception handling allows a program to deal with runtime errors gracefully using try, except, else, and finally blocks without crashing the application.
lessons.expected_output: Enna Kodumai Sir Idhu! Cannot divide by zero!

quiz.question: What block runs when an exception occurs inside a try block?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Sanda nu vandha valikka thaan seiyum, try-except potta error escape aagum!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
Exception handled safely!
quiz.incorrect_text: Goundamani: "Javascript-la 'catch' use panreengala? Python-la 'except' dhaan da use pannanum!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😠
Python uses 'except' block!

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
lessons.subtitle: Superstar Rajini Class & Object (Chitti 2.0)
lessons.tanglish_exp: OOP-na Blueprint and Real Object! class Robot: nguradhu Blueprint template. chitti = Robot("Chitti", 1) nguradhu Real Object instance! __init__() method dhaan object create aagum podhu initial values set panra Constructor!
lessons.english_exp: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) uses Classes as blueprints to instantiate Objects. The __init__() method initializes object properties upon creation.
lessons.initial_code:
class Robot:
    def __init__(self, name, version):
        self.name = name
        self.version = version

chitti = Robot("Chitti", 2.0)
print("Robot Name:", chitti.name)
print("Version:", chitti.version)

quiz.question: Which special method is the constructor in Python classes?
quiz.correct_text: Rajini: "Naan solradhaiyum seiven, __init__ constructor-a correct-a call panniyum seiven!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
__init__ is the constructor king!
quiz.incorrect_text: Chitti: "ERROR: Invalid constructor call! Python classes use __init__ for initialization!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Use __init__(self) method!

lessons.title: List Comprehensions
lessons.subtitle: Fast-Track Banana Counter (Senthil & Goundamani)
lessons.tanglish_exp: For loop-a 4 lines ezhuthaama, single line-la list create panradhu thaan List Comprehension! Syntax: [x * 2 for x in range(5)]. Goundamani: 'Oru ruba kooda line waste pannaama single line-la super loop execution!'
lessons.english_exp: List comprehension offers a shorter syntax when you want to create a new list based on the values of an existing list or sequence. Syntax: [expression for item in iterable if condition].
lessons.initial_code:
# Single line list comprehension!
squares = [x ** 2 for x in range(1, 6)]
print("Squared numbers list:")
print(squares)

quiz.question: What is the output of [x for x in range(3)]?
quiz.correct_text: Goundamani: "Single line-la loop execution fast-a mudichitiye pa! [0, 1, 2]!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
Single line comprehension super!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "range(3) 0-la irundhu 2 varai thaan pogaum anna! 3 வராது!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😅
range(3) generates 0, 1, 2!

lessons.title: Lambda & Anonymous Functions
lessons.subtitle: Vadivelu Quick One-Liner Plan
lessons.tanglish_exp: Name-e illadha small one-line functions thaan Lambda Functions! Syntax: add = lambda a, b: a + b. def build-up illama spot-la quick function operation perform panna Lambda king!
lessons.english_exp: A lambda function is a small anonymous function. A lambda function can take any number of arguments, but can only have one expression. Syntax: lambda arguments : expression.
lessons.initial_code:
# Quick lambda one-liner!
double = lambda x: x * 2
print("Double of 15 is:")
print(double(15))

quiz.question: Which keyword creates an anonymous one-line function in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Def build-up illama spot-la lambda potu 30 calculation get pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😏
Lambda power! Fast calculation!
quiz.incorrect_text: Santhanam: "Lambda-nu ezhuthaama inline-nu ezhuthurane paaru! Inga anonymous function error!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🤦‍♂️
Use 'lambda' keyword!

lessons.title: Map & Filter Functions
lessons.subtitle: Vivek Filter Scheme (Cleaning Bad Elements)
lessons.tanglish_exp: List-la irukra values-a transform panna map(), condition check panni clean-a separate panna filter() use aagum! Vivek: 'Bad elements-a filter panni, positive energy-a map panrom!'
lessons.english_exp: The map() function applies a function to all items in an input list, while filter() creates an iterator of elements for which a function returns True.
lessons.initial_code:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
# Filter even numbers!
evens = list(filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0, numbers))
print("Filtered Even Numbers:")
print(evens)

quiz.question: What does filter() do in Python?
options: Keeps items where condition is True
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "Even numbers-a exact-a filter panni clean-a output eduthutae pa! [2, 4, 6]!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
Filtered elements clean output!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Filter-nguradhu check condition True-va irukra items-a thaan kootitu varum!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
filter() checks boolean condition!

lessons.title: Modules & Importing Libraries
lessons.subtitle: Importing Heavy Weapons (Chitti & Rajini)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Python-la pre-built power-packed modules iruku! import math, import random, import json. External weapons-a import panni heavy calculations-a single line-la compute pannalaam. Chitti: 'Red chip import activated!'
lessons.english_exp: Modules are Python files containing code definitions and statements. Use the import keyword to include built-in or third-party packages like math or random.
lessons.initial_code:
import math

number = 16
print("Square root of 16 is:")
print(int(math.sqrt(number)))

quiz.question: Which keyword includes an external module in your Python script?
quiz.correct_text: Chitti: "Speed 1 Terahertz! Math module-a import panni square root 4 calculated!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
import math success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Goundamani: "C++ include / JS require-naa Inga potu thakkanumnu illa! Python uses 'import'!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😠
Python uses 'import' keyword!

lessons.title: Sets & Unique Collections
lessons.subtitle: Duplicate Item Remover (Santhanam)
lessons.tanglish_exp: List-la duplicate items irukum. Aana Set-la duplicates irukkaadhu! numbers = set([1, 2, 2, 3, 3]) potta automatic-a duplicates clean aayi unique values {1, 2, 3} thaan nikkum!
lessons.english_exp: Sets are unordered collections of unique elements. Sets automatically eliminate duplicate entries and support mathematical set operations like union and intersection.
lessons.initial_code:
raw_list = [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 5]
unique_set = set(raw_list)
print("Unique items:")
print(sorted(list(unique_set)))

quiz.question: What happens when you pass a list with duplicate elements to set()?
quiz.correct_text: Santhanam: "Duplicate items-a set() clean-a தூக்கிடுச்சு! Unique items only!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
Duplicates removed automatically!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "Set-la duplicate items allow aagum-nu nenaikureengala anna? Set contains ONLY unique items!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
set() eliminates duplicate entries!

lessons.title: Decorators (@wrapper)
lessons.subtitle: Vadivelu Build-up Wrapper
lessons.tanglish_exp: Existing function-a modify pannaama surrounding build-up / timing add panradhuthaan Decorator (@my_decorator)! Vadivelu entry-ku munnadi audio build-up podura maadhiri wrapper function execute aagum!
lessons.english_exp: A decorator takes in a function, adds some functionality, and returns it. Syntax uses the @decorator_name annotation above function declarations.

quiz.question: Which symbol is used to apply a decorator to a function in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "@ symbol-a correct-a select panni build-up wrapper execute pannitiye pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
@ decorator syntax correct!
quiz.incorrect_text: Goundamani: "@ symbol podala-na decorator function recognize aagaadhu da!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😠
Use '@decorator_name' syntax!

lessons.title: Generators & Yield Keyword
lessons.subtitle: Endless Tea Track Producer (Goundamani & Senthil)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Whole list-a RAM memory-la store pannaama, onnoonaa values-a stream panna yield keyword use aagum! Memory efficient tea track generator! Senthil single tea as requested!
lessons.english_exp: Generators are functions that return an iterator using the yield keyword, generating items one at a time on-demand without loading the entire sequence into memory.

quiz.question: Which keyword is used inside a generator function to yield values one at a time?
quiz.correct_text: Goundamani: "yield keyword-a use panni memory-ya save panni tea count-a stream pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😛
yield keyword streams data lazily!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "'return' potta function stop aayidum anna! Generator-ku 'yield' thaan pannanum!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Generators use 'yield' keyword!

lessons.title: Working with JSON Data
lessons.subtitle: API Payload Parser (Chitti Memory Unit)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Web APIs & Databases data-va JSON format-la exchange panraanga! Python-la JSON String-a Python Dictionary-a convert panna json.loads(), Python Dictionary-a JSON String-a convert panna json.dumps()!
lessons.english_exp: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a standard data interchange format. Python provides the built-in json module with json.loads() (parse JSON string) and json.dumps() (encode to JSON string).
lessons.initial_code:
import json

json_str = '{"bot": "Chitti", "speed": "1 Terahertz"}'
data = json.loads(json_str)
print("Bot Name:", data["bot"])
print("Speed:", data["speed"])

quiz.question: Which function converts a JSON formatted string into a Python dictionary?
quiz.correct_text: Chitti: "json.loads() call panni JSON string-a Python dictionary-a exact-a parse pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
json.loads() success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "json.dumps() string output kudukum anna! Parsing-ku json.loads() thaan use pannanum!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Use json.loads() for parsing JSON string!

lessons.title: Regular Expressions (RegEx)
lessons.subtitle: Detective Vivek Pattern Finder
lessons.tanglish_exp: Text-kulla specific patterns (e.g. Email, Phone numbers, Keywords) detect panna RegEx (`import re`) use aagum! Detective Vivek: 'Pattern matching-la namma sothukkulle meen adikira maadhiri exact-a match edupom!'
lessons.english_exp: Regular Expressions (RegEx) are sequence of characters that form a search pattern. The re module provides methods like re.search() and re.findall() for text pattern matching.
lessons.initial_code:
import re

text = "Python Kadhai Topic 19"
match = re.search(r"Topic \d+", text)
if match:
    print("Found Pattern:", match.group())

quiz.question: Which Python module is used for Regular Expressions?
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "import re potu pattern 'Topic 19'-a detective maadhiri kandu pudichutae!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
RegEx pattern match success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Python built-in RegEx module name 're' boss! import re-nu type pannunga!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Python built-in module is 're'!

lessons.title: Multithreading & Parallel Execution
lessons.subtitle: Speed 1 Terahertz Parallel Tasks (Chitti 2.0)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Multiple tasks-a simultaneous-a perform panna Multithreading (`import threading`) use aagum! Chitti: 'Naan oru kayyile robot fight, innoru kayyile Python compilation parallel-a panren!'
lessons.english_exp: Multithreading allows a program to run multiple operations concurrently within the same process space using the threading or asyncio modules.
lessons.initial_code:
import threading
import time

def task1():
    print("Task 1: Speed 1 Terahertz Running!")

t = threading.Thread(target=task1)
t.start()
t.join()

quiz.question: Which module allows thread creation for parallel execution in standard Python?
quiz.correct_text: Rajini: "threading module-a correct-a use panni parallel task execution start pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
threading module execution success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Chitti: "ERROR: Module name is 'threading'! Use `import threading` for multithreading!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Standard module name is 'threading'!

lessons.title: Virtual Environments & PIP
lessons.subtitle: Secret Isolated Room (Vadivelu Sandbox)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Project dependencies clash aagaama thadukka Virtual Environment (`venv`) create panrom! Isolated room-kulla pip install package potta global Python clean-a irukum! Vadivelu safe room logic!
lessons.english_exp: A virtual environment (`venv`) is a self-contained directory tree that contains a Python installation for a particular version of Python, plus a number of additional packages.
lessons.initial_code:
# Virtual Environment CLI Simulation
cmd = "python -m venv env"
print("Created isolated environment:")
print(cmd)
lessons.expected_output:
Created isolated environment:
python -m venv env

quiz.question: Which standard module command creates a virtual environment in Python 3?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Isolated room-a create panni global Python-a crash aagaama safe-a poitiye pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
python -m venv env success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "Venv create panna `python -m venv env` thaan command anna!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Use 'python -m venv env'!

lessons.title: Enumerate & Zip Functions
lessons.subtitle: Senthil Double Pairing Machine
lessons.tanglish_exp: List index + value saenthu veonum-na enumerate(list)! Rendu lists-a parallel-a pair panna zip(list1, list2)! Senthil tea & vadai pairing machine maadhiri exact pairs!
lessons.english_exp: enumerate() adds a counter to an iterable and returns it as an enumerate object. zip() takes iterables, aggregates them in a tuple, and returns it.
lessons.initial_code:
names = ["Chitti", "Vadivelu"]
roles = ["Robot", "Rowdy"]
for name, role in zip(names, roles):
    print(f"{name} is a {role}")
lessons.expected_output:
Chitti is a Robot
Vadivelu is a Rowdy

quiz.question: Which function pairs elements from multiple lists in parallel tuples?
quiz.correct_text: Goundamani: "zip() function potu name and role parallel-a pair pannitiye pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😛
zip() function super pairing!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Parallel list pairing-ku `zip()` function thaan use pannanum boss!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Use zip(list1, list2)!

lessons.title: Datetime & Time Operations
lessons.subtitle: Chitti Time Machine & Alarm (Rajini)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Dates, timestamps and delays handle panna import datetime & import time! datetime.now() current system time-a tharum. time.sleep(2) potta execution 2 seconds pause aagum!
lessons.english_exp: The datetime module supplies classes for manipulating dates and times. The time module provides time-related functions like time.sleep() for execution delays.
lessons.initial_code:
from datetime import datetime
now = datetime.now()
print("Current Year:")
print(now.year)

quiz.question: Which function pauses program execution for N seconds in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Rajini: "time.sleep() potu execution delay-a exact-a handle pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
time.sleep(N) delay success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Chitti: "Python uses `time.sleep(seconds)` to delay execution!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Use time.sleep(N)!

lessons.title: Variable Arguments (*args & **kwargs)
lessons.subtitle: Goundamani Unlimited Order Request
lessons.tanglish_exp: Function-ku evlo parameters varum-nu fix panna mudiyaadha podhu *args (tuple of arguments) and **kwargs (dictionary of key-value arguments) use panrom! Goundamani unlimited tea order request handle panra template!
lessons.english_exp: *args allows a function to accept any number of positional arguments. **kwargs allows a function to accept any number of keyword arguments as a dictionary.
lessons.expected_output:
Order: Tea
Order: Vadai
Order: Bun

quiz.question: Which parameter syntax accepts unlimited keyword arguments into a dictionary?
quiz.correct_text: Goundamani: "**kwargs potu key-value arguments-a dictionary-a catch pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
**kwargs dictionary arguments!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "*args positional tuple-ku, **kwargs dictionary key-values-ku anna!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Use **kwargs for keyword args!

lessons.title: Shallow vs Deep Copy
lessons.subtitle: Anniyan Identity Duplicate (Vikram)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Direct assignment b = a potta reference copy dhaan aagum. Outer copy-ku copy.copy() (Shallow), nested list deep copy-ku copy.deepcopy()! Anniyan identity duplicate modification safety!
lessons.english_exp: A shallow copy constructs a new compound object and inserts references into it. A deep copy constructs a new object and recursively inserts copies of objects found in the original.
lessons.initial_code:
import copy
original = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
deep = copy.deepcopy(original)
deep[0][0] = 99
print("Original stays untouched:", original[0][0])
print("Deep copy modified:", deep[0][0])
lessons.expected_output:
Original stays untouched: 1
Deep copy modified: 99

quiz.question: Which function recursively duplicates nested objects independently?
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "copy.deepcopy() pota original list untouched-a iruku! Perfect isolation!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
copy.deepcopy() recursive isolation!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "Shallow copy potta nested list reference inner change original-aiyum maathidum boss!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😰
Use copy.deepcopy() for nested objects!

lessons.title: Packing & Unpacking
lessons.subtitle: Vadivelu Luggage Bag Unpacker
lessons.tanglish_exp: Multiple values-a single line-la variables-ku distribute panradhu Unpacking! Syntax: a, b, *rest = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Vadivelu luggage bag unpack panra maadhiri clean variable distribution!
lessons.english_exp: Unpacking allows extracting elements of an iterable into multiple variables in a single line. Use `*var` to capture remaining elements into a list.
lessons.initial_code:
numbers = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
first, second, *rest = numbers
print("First:", first)
print("Second:", second)
print("Rest list:", rest)

quiz.question: What is stored in 'rest' after a, *rest = [1, 2, 3]?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "*rest potu remaining values [2, 3]-a list-a catch pannitiye pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
*rest captures remaining list!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "* symbol potta dhaan remaining items list-a collect aagum anna!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Use *variable for remaining items!

lessons.title: Web Scraping & Requests
lessons.subtitle: Detective Vivek Web Spy
lessons.tanglish_exp: Internet web pages & APIs-la irundhu data fetch panna HTTP requests use panrom (`import urllib.request` or `requests`)! Detective Vivek web spy style-la URL status 200 OK verify panrom!
lessons.english_exp: Python uses HTTP libraries like urllib.request or requests to communicate with web servers and APIs over HTTP/HTTPS protocols.
lessons.initial_code:
import urllib.parse
url = "https://example.com/api?q=python"
parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
print("Scheme:", parsed.scheme)
print("Domain:", parsed.netloc)

quiz.question: What HTTP status code indicates a successful web request response?
quiz.correct_text: Vivekh: "200 OK status get panni request success-a fetch pannitiye pa!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
HTTP 200 OK success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Vadivelu: "404 Not Found / 500 Server Error da! 200 thaan success status!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
200 OK represents success!

lessons.title: CSV File Processing
lessons.subtitle: Santhanam Excel Data Reader
lessons.tanglish_exp: Tabular Excel / Sheet data-va read/write panna import csv module use aagum! csv.reader(file) rows-a list-a parse panni fast data inspection panradhu Santhanam style!
lessons.english_exp: The csv module implements classes to read and write tabular data in CSV (Comma Separated Values) format.
lessons.initial_code:
import csv
# Simulating CSV row parsing
csv_data = [["Name", "Role"], ["Vadivelu", "Rowdy"], ["Chitti", "Robot"]]
for row in csv_data[1:]:
    print(f"{row[0]}: {row[1]}")

quiz.question: Which Python module handles CSV reading and writing?
quiz.correct_text: Santhanam: "import csv module potu rows-a clean-a parse pannitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😂
import csv success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Senthil: "Built-in module name 'csv' thaan anna! import csv nu pannanum!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
🥺
Use 'import csv'!

lessons.title: Closures & Inner Functions
lessons.subtitle: Nested Secret Plan Generator (Vadivelu)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Outer function execution mudinjalum inner function outer variables-a memory-la remember panni vechirukum! Idhudhaan Closure! Vadivelu nested secret plan memory!
lessons.english_exp: A closure is a function object that remembers values in enclosing scopes even if they are not present in memory.

quiz.question: What feature allows an inner function to remember variables from its enclosing outer scope?
quiz.correct_text: Vadivelu: "Outer function mudinjum 'Escape Strategy'-a memory-la Remember pannichi! Closure power!"
quiz.correct_svg:
😎
Closure scope memory success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Goundamani: "Inner function outer scope variable-a retain panradhu Closure da!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😠
This concept is called Closure!

lessons.title: Type Hinting & Annotations
lessons.subtitle: Professional Code Inspector (Chitti 3.0)
lessons.tanglish_exp: Professional enterprise Python code-la parameters and return types-a clear-a specify panna Type Annotations use panrom: def add(x: int, y: int) -> int:. Chitti 3.0 Code Inspector approved!
lessons.english_exp: Python type hinting (introduced in PEP 484) allows developers to specify expected data types for function parameters and return values.
lessons.initial_code:
def calculate_total(price: float, tax: float) -> float:
    return price + tax

total = calculate_total(100.0, 18.0)
print("Total Amount:", total)

quiz.question: Which syntax specifies a function return type hint in Python?
quiz.correct_text: Chitti: "Speed 1 Terahertz! `-> float` return type hint potu professional code ezhuthitiye!"
quiz.correct_svg:
🤖
-> type annotation success!
quiz.incorrect_text: Chitti: "Python function return type hint uses `-> ReturnType` syntax!"
quiz.incorrect_svg:
😵
Use `def func() -> ReturnType:`!

chatbot_meme_responses.response: Vanakkam! Naan thaan Chitti RAG 3.0, Speed 1 Terahertz, Memory 1 Zettabyte! 🤖 Python 30 complete curriculum topics documents-a RAG retrieval model-la search panni, memes mood-la instant-a response solidaren!

Variables & Data Types pathi ketrukeenga! Namma Goundamani & Senthil Banana comedy-ya vechiye dynamic-a solidaren. Variable-nguradhu namma pocket maadhiri values store panni vechira box. E.g., banana_count = 2. Integer, Float, String, and Boolean values easily stored! 🍌

Conditionals (If-Else) pathi puriya veikuren! Vadivelu 'Naanum Rowdy Thaan' templates control flow match panni easy-a purinjiklaam. Condition true-na if block execute aagum, false-na else block! Indentation space (4 spaces) miss panna IndentationError varum! 👮‍♂️

Loops (for / while) matrum range() control parameters pathi solidaren! Thirumba thirumba actions perform panni repeat execution panna loops use aagum. Vivekh tea shop beats maadhiri repeat execution! 🌀
Functions matrum def logic templates parse pannalaam! Functions-nguradhu reusable code block template. Vadivelu attack plan create panna def attack_plan(): define panni call pannanum! 📢
Lists & Dictionaries collections pathi easy-a details parse pannalam! List elements index numbers start from 0: menu[0] = tea! Dictionary key-value format: user["role"] = Rowdy! ☕
Strings & Slicing pathi ketrukeenga! Vadivelu comedy style-la text reversal text[::-1] potta string reverse aayidum! text.upper() potta Vadivelu shouting text ALL CAPS-la maarum! 🧵
File Handling pathi solidaren! Vadivelu secret document reader logic: with open('file.txt', 'r') as f:. `with` statement use panna operation mudinjadhum file automatically close aayidum! 📁

Exception Handling (Try-Except) pathi ketrukeenga! Prabhu: 'Enna kodumai sir idhu!' Code crash aagaama handle panna try & except block use aagum. Divide by zero error-a handle panna except ZeroDivisionError:! 🛡️

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) pathi ketrukeenga! Superstar Rajini / Chitti style-la: class Robot: nguradhu Blueprint, chitti = Robot("Chitti") nguradhu Real Object! __init__() dhaan Constructor method! 🤖
List Comprehensions (Topic 11) pathi ketrukeenga! For loop-a single line-la ezhuthalaam: [x * 2 for x in range(5)]. Goundamani fast-track loop execution! ⚡
Lambda Functions (Topic 12) pathi ketrukeenga! Name-e illadha small one-line functions: add = lambda a, b: a + b. Spot-la quick function execution! 🎯
Map & Filter Functions (Topic 13) pathi solidaren! Vivek filter scheme style-la list elements-a condition check panni clean-a separate panna filter(lambda x: x > 5, list)! 🧹
Modules & Importing (Topic 14) pathi ketrukeenga! External heavy weapons import: import math, import random, import json. Chitti red chip import activated! 📦
Sets & Unique Collections (Topic 15) pathi solidaren! List-la duplicates remove panna set(list) potta Santhanam automatic duplicate item remover maadhiri unique values thaan nirkum! 💎
Decorators (@wrapper) (Topic 16) pathi ketrukeenga! Existing function-ku Vadivelu audio entry build-up wrapper add panradhu thaan Decorator (@buildup_decorator)! 🎭
Generators & Yield (Topic 17) pathi solidaren! RAM memory-la whole list load pannaama, values-a yield keyword valiya lazy-a stream panna Generators king! 🌀
JSON Handling (Topic 18) pathi ketrukeenga! JSON String-a Python Dictionary-a convert panna json.loads(), Python Dictionary-a JSON String-a convert panna json.dumps()! 📄
Regular Expressions (RegEx) (Topic 19) pathi solidaren! Detective Vivek pattern finder style-la text-kulla patterns search panna import re matrum re.search(r'pattern', text)! 🔍
Multithreading & Async (Topic 20) pathi ketrukeenga! Multiple operations-a parallel-a run panna import threading. Chitti Speed 1 Terahertz parallel tasks running! ⚡
Virtual Environments & PIP (Topic 21) pathi ketrukeenga! Project dependencies clash aagaama isolated sandbox create panna: python -m venv env! 🏰
Enumerate & Zip (Topic 22) pathi solidaren! Index+Value pair-ku enumerate(list), two lists parallel pair-ku zip(list1, list2)! 🤝
Datetime & Time (Topic 23) pathi ketrukeenga! System time-ku datetime.now(), execution delay-ku time.sleep(seconds)! ⏰
*args & **kwargs (Topic 24) pathi solidaren! Unlimited positional arguments-ku *args, dictionary keyword args-ku **kwargs! 🎁
Shallow vs Deep Copy (Topic 25) pathi ketrukeenga! Nested objects-a independent-a duplicate panna copy.deepcopy()! 👥
Packing & Unpacking (Topic 26) pathi solidaren! List values-a single line variables-ku distribute panna: a, b, *rest = list! 🧳
Web Requests & Scraping (Topic 27) pathi ketrukeenga! Web pages & APIs communicate panna urllib.request. Status 200 OK means success! 🌐
CSV File Processing (Topic 28) pathi solidaren! Excel & tabular sheet rows parse panna import csv and csv.reader()! 📊
Closures (Topic 29) pathi ketrukeenga! Outer scope variables-a memory-la remember panra inner function thaan Closure! 🔒
Type Hinting (Topic 30) pathi solidaren! Professional code-la parameters and return types specify panna: def func(x: int) -> str:! 🏷️
🐛 Debugger Helper: IndentationError! Vadivelu space master says: 'Space poduradhulaye gavanama irukanum boss!'. Python-la if, for, or def-ku kela 4 spaces (Tab) exact-a align pannanum!
🐛 Debugger Helper: NameError! Senthil: 'Variable name-a create-e pannama access panreengala anna?!'. Check spelling or define variable before calling it: x = 10!
🐛 Debugger Helper: TypeError! Vadivelu: 'String-aiyum Integer-aiyum direct-a sethu add panna mudiyaadhu da!'. Convert number to string first using str(number)!
🐛 Debugger Helper: ZeroDivisionError! Goundamani: 'Enna da 0-vaala divide panra? Math-la idhu illegal!'. Use try...except ZeroDivisionError: block to catch it!
🐛 Debugger Helper: IndexError! Senthil: 'List length 3 thaan anna! Neenga 5th index ketta bun varuma?!'. Remember list index starts at 0 up to len(list)-1!
🐛 Debugger Helper: KeyError! Vadivelu: 'Dictionary-la illadha key-a request panreengale pa!'. Use dict.get('key') to safely handle missing keys!
🐛 Debugger Helper: SyntaxError! Vivekh: 'Colon (:) podala, illana bracket close panna marandhuteenga!'. Check your colons at the end of `if`, `for`, `def` lines!
🐛 Debugger Helper: AttributeError! Santhanam: 'Integer-ku split() method call panra paaru! Enna puthisaalithanam!'. Verify methods belong to the correct object data type!
🐛 Debugger Helper: ModuleNotFoundError! Chitti: 'Module not installed or spelling wrong!'. Run pip install package_name in terminal!
🐛 Debugger Helper: ValueError! Vadivelu: 'Text "abc"-ya `int("abc")`-nu integer-a convert panna mudiyuma?!'. Convert string numbers only (e.g. `int("123")`)!
Vadivelu Meme Classic: 'Sanda nu vandha valikka thaan seiyum, code nu ezhudhuna error vara thaan seiyum!' 🤪 Keep debugging, mass pannaam!
Goundamani & Senthil Classic: 'Rendu banana-la onna saptutu mathadhu enga nu ketta logic illa nu solra?!' 🍌 Master Python variable logic first!
Superstar Rajini / Chitti Classic: 'Naan solradhaiyum seiven, solladhadhaiyum seiven! Speed 1 Terahertz, Memory 1 Zettabyte!' 🤖 Red chip activated!
Vivekh Comedy Classic: 'Aanandam... vilaiyaadum veedu! Innuma indha ooru loop-a nambitu iruku?' 🌀 Write clean Python code!
Sana... Sana... En Sana! 🤖 ERROR 404: Emotion core logic memory crash! Red chip activated! Naan solradhaiyum seiven, solladhadhaiyum seiven! Let's code Python instead! 🔴
Python syntax checks dynamic simple codes! English maadhiri clean-a write pannalaam. Life set-u! 🐍
